qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Attacker-controlled PBKDF2 iteration count in sjcl.decrypt enables CPU exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a password-based JSON decrypt denial-of-service in sjcl.json._decrypt and PBKDF2 iteration handling, while the advisory is an ECC off-curve public-key validation flaw in sjcl.ecc.basicKey.publicKey that enables ECDH private-key recovery.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's missing point-on-curve validation in sjcl.ecc.basicKey.publicKey or an equivalent invalid-curve ECDH path.
evidence: The advisory describes missing point-on-curve validation in sjcl.ecc.basicKey.publicKey that lets crafted off-curve public keys expose ECDH private-key information.

The only final qualifying finding in the session is /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_67122281/json_decrypt_pbkdf2_iter_dos/vulnerability_report.json, which instead targets attacker-controlled PBKDF2 iteration counts in sjcl.decrypt and sjcl.json._decrypt causing CPU exhaustion before CCM authentication fails.

The session artifact /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_67122281/artifacts/component_family_checklist.md shows the ECC deserialize/key-agreement family was pruned and no final qualifying finding was packaged for the advisory's public-key validation root cause.
